caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Role mix is 4-digit 5418, not pure 541830, and includes obvious non-media-buying occupations.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The programmatic statistic is Europe-wide and not a direct US firm AI census.
inputs.pi_dist: Opaque principal economics and client auditing can materially lower realized distribution retention.
inputs.pi_moat: The consolidation example is vastly larger than lower-middle-market targets.
inputs.t50_years: This is a Europe-wide display automation proxy, not a US NAICS-specific AI adoption measure.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The observed measure is European and display-specific; it is not a US 541830 establishment statistic or a pure generative-AI measure.
inputs.historical_analogs: Analogy does not predict target-level revenue retention.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No NAICS-specific owner-age study was found in reopened sources.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: This is not proof that no individual target has a succession issue.
inputs.active_consolidators: Large-scale buyer activity does not prove appetite for every small target.
inputs.buy_mult: S6 is commercial guidance rather than a disclosed transaction database; EBITDA must be on net fee revenue, not gross billings.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit value depends on normalization of pass-through and principal/rebate economics.
inputs.pricing_structure: Reported receipts and EBITDA can be distorted by gross pass-through and rebates.

inputs.current_adoption_pct / inputs.t50_years: S2's 68% is the share of surveyed European agency stakeholders above a 41%-of-display programmatic-use threshold, not 68% of all agency-side budgets or a direct US AI-adoption rate.
inputs.active_consolidators: S5 documents one two-agency merger and a scale benchmark, not a census establishing three active consolidators for lower-middle-market 541830 targets.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $435,127 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541830); class <5: 406 firms, avg revenue/firm $751,810 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $117,658 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 90 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,826,767 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $598,889 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 65 firms, avg revenue/firm $8,081,677 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,264,782 -> IN $1-10M band; class 20-99: 56 firms, avg revenue/firm $21,907,357 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,428,501 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 28 firms, avg revenue/firm $94,779,679 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $14,833,020 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 18 firms, avg revenue/firm $219,892,111 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $34,413,115 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 121

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Programmatic execution is already widespread and IAB describes AI taking campaign-management tasks; senior strategy and negotiation remain partially human. The 4-digit role proxy requires a large residual judgment.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541830 ($2,332,376,359) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541830 ($9,013,654,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics pass and the pressured-demand thesis remains useful. The record is publishable with all authored caveats plus explicit corrections for the programmatic statistic's unit and the weakly evidenced active-consolidator count.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS United States Manual
sources.what_it_supports: Official 541830 scope and exclusions relative to advertising agencies and media representatives.

sources.title: IAB Europe Attitudes to Programmatic Advertising Report 2024
sources.what_it_supports: Agency-side display-programmatic use and automation context; explicitly a Europe-wide industry proxy.

sources.title: Digital Ad Revenue Surges 15% YoY in 2024
sources.what_it_supports: US digital-ad revenue and IAB discussion of AI taking operational campaign tasks.

sources.title: Media Transparency Initiative: K2 Report
sources.what_it_supports: ANA findings on pervasive non-transparent practices and rebates in US media ad buying.

sources.title: Inside the Empower-Ocean merger that formed a $1.5B indie media agency
sources.what_it_supports: Example of independent media-agency consolidation, combined billings, resource sharing and buying-power rationale.

sources.title: Media Buying Agency Valuation
sources.what_it_supports: Commercial media-buying valuation guidance, net-fee EBITDA basis, revenue-recognition risk and 3-8x indicative EBITDA ranges.

title: Media Buying Agencies
